intent: Give every device on the LAN the same domain blocking as the PC's /etc/hosts, with no app installed on the device, by running the PC as a Pi-hole-like DNS (and, for this router, DHCP) server.
scope: linux_configuration/scripts/single_use/features/setup_dns_blocker.sh (new: dnsmasq-based LAN DNS blocker + optional DHCP-server mode)
linux_configuration/scripts/single_use/features/setup_wireguard_ssh.sh (new allow-dns subcommand: opens DNS 53 + DHCP 67 for the LAN in the single-owner nftables ruleset)
Non-goals: no Pi-hole/web dashboard, no per-query analytics, no DoH enforcement on clients (a voluntary-DNS design cannot force off-path traffic).
changes: dnsmasq serves the identical blocklist as /etc/hosts via addn-hosts pointed at generate_hosts_file.sh output (same StevenBlack variant + custom entries + unblocks); no-hosts + no-resolv keep it deterministic; bound to the LAN interface only so the systemd-resolved stub stays free.
Daily systemd timer rebuilds the feed and SIGHUPs dnsmasq; Restart=always drop-in; logs to /var/log/dns-blocker/.

dhcp/dhcp-off subcommands: because the ISP router cannot advertise a custom DNS, the PC becomes the LAN DHCP server (static IP via nmcli with a gateway-ping self-check + auto-revert; hands out itself as DNS). Rollback keeps the PC online on its static IP.

Firewall: allow-dns opens udp/tcp 53 (LAN saddr) and udp 67 for DHCP. The DHCP accept rule is placed BEFORE 'ct state invalid drop' because the 0.0.0.0->255.255.255.255 broadcast is classified INVALID and was being dropped.
Fixed firewall detection to read the actual loaded nftables ruleset (a ruleset can be loaded while nftables.service reads inactive), avoiding a grep -q/pipefail SIGPIPE false-negative.

verification.command: sudo setup_dns_blocker.sh setup && sudo setup_dns_blocker.sh dhcp && setup_dns_blocker.sh status
verification.evidence: status all green: dnsmasq active/enabled, listening 192.168.1.43:53, firewall DNS/DHCP open for LAN, DHCP mode ON. Pixel-6a leased 192.168.1.82 with option6 dns-server=192.168.1.43 (DHCPDISCOVER->OFFER->REQUEST->ACK). dig @192.168.1.43 youtube.com -> 0.0.0.0 (blocked); example.com/wikipedia.org -> real IP.
User confirmed: legit websites load, youtube.com is blocked.
PASS: dispatch (help lists all subcommands, unknown cmd exits non-zero) + firewall_is_loaded regression (drop->LOADED, accept->NOT_LOADED under set -o pipefail with large nft output).

risks: Voluntary DNS: a device using DoH / Android Private DNS / a VPN / a manual DNS bypasses blocking; the PC is not the gateway so it cannot force traffic. Phone Private DNS must be Off.
PC is now the LAN DHCP server: if it is down, new devices get no lease (existing leases persist ~12h). Fail-closed by design.
Static IP pinned on enp6s0; a wrong gateway would break connectivity (mitigated by an immediate ping self-check that auto-reverts).
rollback: DHCP: sudo setup_dns_blocker.sh dhcp-off (stops serving DHCP, KEEPS the PC on its static IP so it stays online), then re-tick the router's DHCP.
DNS blocker: systemctl disable --now dnsmasq dns-blocklist-refresh.timer; rm /etc/dnsmasq.d/lan-blocker.conf /etc/dnsmasq.d/lan-dhcp.conf.
Validate after rollback: devices get leases from the router again and normal DNS resolves.
